title: medicago sativa subsp. sativa
intro: Medicago sativa subsp. sativa, also known as common alfalfa or lucerne, is a perennial legume plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its saponin and flavonoid content, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Medicago sativa subsp. sativa include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Alfalfa is often consumed as a herbal tea, capsule, or powder, and is also used in traditional medicine to make tinctures and infusions.
